description:
[EPIC] Replace per-callsite spec minting with one canonical spec per fn,
keyed on the LUB (element-wise Descr::union) of every observed input.
Track per-slot inflow histograms alongside the LUB.

This ticket is the brief. Execution lives in three children that form
a linear DAG — each lands tests-green on its own.

## DAG

  fz-a5z (.1) ──blocks─→ fz-b9b (.2) ──blocks─→ fz-vy7 (.3)

  .1  Canonical model + LUB observation (additive; no goldens change)
  .2  Demolition: collapse to one spec per fn (regen 100 goldens; measure)
  .3  Diagnostic (--Wspec-quality) + close fz-t45 + sweep

## The model

Canonical { fid, slots: Vec<CanonicalSlot> }
CanonicalSlot { descr: Descr, inflows: HashMap<ArgRepr, usize> }

The canonical IS the type-theoretic soundness floor. Where callers
are monomorphic, the LUB stays narrow and ArgRepr projects to
RawInt/RawF64 naturally. Where callers mix, the LUB widens to Tagged.
No separate refinement machinery, no router, no per-target emission
policy in v1.

## Why the histogram now

One mechanism, three consumers (only #1 active in v1):

1. **Correctness invariant** — every contributing ArgRepr in a slot's
   inflows must project from a Descr ⊆ the slot's LUB. Stats prove
   the LUB construction is consistent. Debug-assert.

2. **Refinement-emission trigger (deferred)** — slot with dominant
   narrower-repr inflow → mint a refinement keyed on that repr.

3. **Inlining candidate (further deferred)** — singleton-count caller
   is an inline candidate.

Each downstream consumer reads the same statistic. No re-architecture
when we flip (2) or (3) on.

## Expected effect

CLIF function count drops to ~1 per fn + helper conts:
- fib_tailrec: 14 → ~5
- ast_eval_env: 59 → ~10

fixture_matrix debug wall: 60s → ≤ 20s.

CLIF goldens shrink by majority. Same goldens for JIT and AOT.

## What we remove (as important as what we add)

- `fn ensure_any_keys`.
- `naturally_opaque: HashSet<FnId>` + snapshot logic.
- `any_key_specs: HashMap<FnId, Vec<Descr>>`.
- `tagged_return_specs`, `tagged_slot0_cont_specs`, `tagged_return_fns`
  HashMaps + their propagation loops.
- MakeClosure-target blanket reachable seed in `reachable_specs`.
- Two-tier `SpecRegistry::resolve` (subsumption fallback).
- The parallel any-key body story — canonical IS the any-key.
- Per-callsite key reconstruction at codegen emit time.
- Per-callsite specs HashMap composite keying.
- ~64 references across ir_typer + ir_codegen disappear with the
  bookkeeping maps.

## fz-t45 dissolves

Closure values' body_fn_id wires to canonical's FuncId. Indirect-
dispatch sig is all-Tagged i64. Canonical ABI for opaque-reachable
fns is Tagged by construction (LUB includes opaque callers). Wire
matches semantics. TailCallClosure ABI mismatch becomes structurally
impossible. Closed in fz-7ot.3.

## Deferred (file only when measurable need arises)

- Refinement emission driven by inflow histogram (consumer #2).
- Inlining driven by singleton inflow counts (consumer #3).
- Dispatch-prelude router on canonical for opaque entry into narrow
  refinements.
- Per-target (JIT vs AOT) emission policy and golden split.

These all read the histogram. No re-architecture needed when they ship.

## Children

- fz-a5z — fz-7ot.1 — canonical model + LUB observation (additive)
- fz-b9b — fz-7ot.2 — demolition
- fz-vy7 — fz-7ot.3 — diagnostic + close fz-t45 + sweep

comments.text: Epic halted after fz-a5z (landed). fz-b9b (demolition) attempted full typer-side collapse and surfaced architectural couplings the design missed — multi-clause dispatch, tail-call ABI, and the tagged_return_specs propagation depend on per-callsite spec narrowing in ways that don't survive a naive collapse. WIP preserved in stash. Next iteration: re-approach as codegen-side collapse (one body per FnId emitted from the typer's existing per-callsite spec set) rather than typer-side rewrite. fz-a5z's canonical/histogram machinery remains landed and feeds whatever the next approach looks like.

title: fz-rfn: canonical-first — one spec per fn, LUB-keyed
type: fz-rfn [epic]: canonical-first — one spec per fn, LUB-keyed
